_label: Beschrijvende catalogus der historische verzameling van natuurkundige instrumenten in het Natuurkundig Laboratorium der Rijks-Universiteit te Leiden

identified_by.content: Beschrijvende catalogus der historische verzameling van natuurkundige instrumenten in het Natuurkundig Laboratorium der Rijks-Universiteit te Leiden

referred_to_by.content: This is the founding catalog of what would become **Rijksmuseum Boerhaave** — the Netherlands' national museum of science history. Written by Claude August Crommelin (1878–1965), adjunct-director of the Kamerlingh Onnes Laboratory in Leiden, it systematically documents the historical collection of 18th-century physics instruments that had been displaced by Heike Kamerlingh Onnes's modern cryogenics equipment. The catalog describes demonstration apparatus from the famous **'s Gravesande–Musschenbroek collection** — instruments used to introduce Newtonian physics to continental Europe. Published in the year of Kamerlingh Onnes's death (1926), it directly led to the founding of the Nederlandsch Historisch Natuurwetenschappelijk Museum in 1928, which Albert Einstein visited in its opening year (1931).
